title: Road construction operatives
description: Lay and bind building materials, such as brick, structural tile, concrete block, cinder block, glass block, and terra-cotta block, with mortar and other substances, to construct or repair walls, partitions, arches, sewers, and other structures. Smooth and finish surfaces of poured concrete, such as floors, walks, sidewalks, roads, or curbs using a variety of hand and power tools. Align forms for sidewalks, curbs, or gutters; patch voids; and use saws to cut expansion joints. Apply a mixture of cement, sand, pigment, or marble chips to floors, stairways, and cabinet fixtures to fashion durable and decorative surfaces. (Based on 21 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Learn and follow safety regulations.
Operate equipment including compactors, scrapers, rollers, and machines that clean or cut expansion joints in concrete or asphalt.
Align machines, cutterheads, or depth gauge markers with reference stakes and guidelines, and move equipment according to hand signals from crew members.
Load and move dirt, rocks, equipment, or other materials using various heavy equipment such as trucks, tractors, and cranes.
Repair and maintain equipment, making adjustments or assisting with major repairs as necessary.
Signal operators to guide the movement and alignment of machines or equipment and control traffic near work zones.
Clean, lubricate, and refill equipment as part of maintenance procedures.
Conduct pre-operational checks on equipment and ensure proper functioning before use.
Operate equipment to demolish, remove debris, or manage snow from roads and parking lots.
Dig ditches and trenches, backfill excavations, and compact and level earth according to grade specifications.
Read and follow plans, instructions, or specifications to determine work activities and layout areas for construction work.
Construct and secure forms for pouring concrete and assist in raising them to the required elevation.
Smooth or finish freshly poured cement or concrete using tools such as floats and trowels.
Operate various machines to spread, level, and maintain asphalt, stone, or other materials for road beds.
Erect scaffolding, shoring, or braces when required for installations or repairs.
Mix mortar, plaster, and grout and apply them using appropriate techniques for installations.
Perform support duties to assist masons, plasterers, roofers, or related crafts while adhering to safety regulations.
Position and hold timbers, lumber, or paneling in place for fastening or cutting, and transport tools, materials, equipment, and supplies to work site by hand, handtruck, or heavy, motorized truck.
Examine brickwork or structure to determine need for repair, and clean working surface to remove scale, dust, soot, or chips of brick and mortar, using broom, wire brush, or scraper.

Disassemble and remove damaged or worn pipe, and perform semi-skilled and unskilled laboring duties related to the installation, maintenance and repair of a wide variety of electrical systems and equipment.
Drill holes and pull or push wiring through openings, using hand and power tools, and thread conduit ends, connect couplings, and fabricate and secure conduit support brackets, using hand tools.
Maintain tools, vehicles, and equipment, and keep parts and supplies in order, and perform preventative maintenance on vehicles and heavy equipment.

Measure, cut, and bend wire and conduit, using measuring instruments and hand tools, and examine electrical units for loose connections and broken insulation and tighten connections, using hand tools.

Remove burned or damaged brick or mortar, using sledgehammer, crowbar, chipping gun, or chisel, and assist pipe fitters in the layout, assembly, and installation of piping for air, ammonia, gas, and water systems.
Set the forms that hold concrete to the desired pitch and depth, and align them; signal truck driver to position truck to facilitate pouring concrete, and move chute to direct concrete on forms.
Spread, level, and smooth concrete, using rake, shovel, hand or power trowel, hand or power screed, and float, and mold expansion joints and edges, using edging tools, jointers, and straightedge.

Monitor how the wind, heat, or cold affect the curing of the concrete throughout the entire process, and apply hardening and sealing compounds to cure surface of concrete, and waterproof or restore surface.

Wet concrete surface and rub with stone to smooth surface and obtain specified finish, and sprinkle colored marble or stone chips, powdered steel, or coloring powder over surface to produce prescribed finish.
Cut pipe and lift up to fitters; clean work area and wash parts, and sweep sand from the surface prior to opening to traffic; handle slides, mud, or pit cleanings or maintenance.

Prepare base for installation by removing unstable or unsuitable materials, compacting and grading the soil, draining or stabilizing weak or saturated soils and taking measures to prevent water penetration and migration of bedding sand, and supply and place base materials, edge restraints, bedding sand and jointing sand.
Set pavers, aligning and spacing them correctly, and compact bedding sand and pavers to finish the paved area, using a plate compactor.

Provide instructions regarding material movement or excavation; observe hand signals, grade stakes, or other markings when operating machines so that work can be performed to specifications; drive heavy equipment and vehicles with adjustable attachments to sweep debris from paved surfaces.
Adjust controls of machines that spread, shape, raise, level, or align track, according to specifications, and lubricate machines, change oil, or fill hydraulic reservoirs to specified levels.
Remove litter and debris from roadways, including debris from rock and mud slides; perform roadside landscaping work, such as clearing weeds and brush, and planting and trimming trees.
Set out signs and cones around work areas to divert traffic and flag motorists to warn them of obstacles or repair work ahead; discuss the design with the client.
Drive machines to work sites and inspect equipment prior to operation; create or maintain inclines or ramps, and dig trenches or holes for installation of conduit or supports.
Requisition tools and equipment, select type and size of pipe, and collect and transport materials and equipment to work site; requisition materials, using warehouse requisition or release forms.
Install gas burners to convert furnaces from wood, coal, or oil; maintain tools, vehicles, and equipment, and keep parts and supplies in order.
Engage mechanisms that lay tracks or rails to specified gauges; lubricate, adjust, or repair machinery and replace parts, such as gears, bearings, or bucket teeth.
Inspect markers to verify accurate installation, and paint traffic control lines and place pavement traffic messages, by hand or using machines.
Measure and mark locations for installation of markers, using tape, string, or chalk; cut paving stones to size and for edges, using a splitter and a masonry saw.
Cut metal division strips, and press them into terrazzo base so that top edges form desired design or pattern; adjust dig face angles for varying overburden depths and set lengths.
Drill holes through rails, tie plates, or fishplates for insertion of bolts or spikes, using power drills.
Grind ends of new or worn rails to attain smooth joints, using portable grinders.
Operate track wrenches to tighten or loosen bolts at joints that hold ends of rails together.
Observe leveling indicator arms to verify levelness and alignment of tracks.
Operate single- or multiple-head spike driving machines to drive spikes into ties and secure rails.
Repair or adjust track switches, using wrenches and replacement parts.
Clean or make minor repairs to machines or equipment.
Clean, grade, or level ballast on railroad tracks.
Drive graders, tamping machines, brooms, or ballast spreading machines to redistribute gravel or ballast between rails.
Operate single- or multiple-head spike pullers to pull old spikes from ties.
Drive vehicles that automatically move and lay tracks or rails over sections of track to be constructed, repaired, or maintained.
Turn wheels of machines, using lever controls, to adjust guidelines for track alignments or grades, following specifications.
Spray ties, fishplates, or joints with oil to protect them from weathering.
Push controls to close grasping devices on track or rail sections so that they can be raised or moved.
String and attach wire-guidelines machine to rails so that tracks or rails can be aligned or leveled.
Operate tie-adzing machines to cut ties and permit insertion of fishplates that hold rails.
Paint railroad signs, such as speed limits or gate-crossing warnings.
Clean tracks or clear ice or snow from tracks or switch boxes.
Patrol assigned track sections to locate and report damaged or broken track.
Erect, install, or repair guardrails, road shoulders, berms, highway markers, warning signals, and highway lighting, using hand tools and power tools.
Haul and spread sand, gravel, and clay to fill washouts and repair road shoulders.
Inspect, clean, and repair drainage systems, bridges, tunnels, and other structures.
Drive trucks to transport crews and equipment to work sites.
Dump, spread, and tamp asphalt, using pneumatic tampers, to repair joints and patch broken pavement.
Dress and reshape worn or damaged railroad switch points or frogs, using portable power grinders.
Dump and tamp clay in molds, using tamping tools.
Spread mortar on stopper heads and rods, using trowels, and slide brick sleeves over rods to form refractory jackets.
Reline or repair ladles and pouring spouts with refractory clay, using trowels.
Chip slag from linings of ladles or remove linings when beyond repair, using hammers and chisels.
Mix specified amounts of sand, clay, mortar powder, and water to form refractory clay or mortar, using shovels or mixing machines.
Measure furnace walls to determine dimensions and cut required number of sheets from plastic block, using saws.
Dry and bake new linings by placing inverted linings over burners, building fires in ladles, or by using blowtorches.
Remove worn or damaged plastic block refractory linings of furnaces, using hand tools.
Climb scaffolding, carrying hoses, and spray surfaces of cupolas with refractory mixtures, using spray equipment.
Transfer clay structures to curing ovens, melting tanks, and drawing kilns, using forklifts.

tools_used.name: Front and back shovel wheel loaders

detailed_work_activities.title: Move construction or extraction materials to locations where they are needed.
Assist skilled construction or extraction personnel.
Clean surfaces in preparation for work activities.
Operate heavy-duty construction or installation equipment.
Mix substances or compounds needed for work activities.
Compact materials to create level bases.
Apply sealants or other protective coatings.
Smooth surfaces with abrasive materials or tools.
Install electrical components, equipment, or systems.
Apply material to fill gaps in surfaces.
Operate equipment or vehicles to clear construction sites or move materials.
Mark reference points on construction materials.
Spread concrete or other aggregate mixtures.
Remove worn, damaged or outdated materials from work areas.
Pour materials into or on designated areas.
Break up rock, asphalt, or concrete.
Spread sand, dirt or other loose materials onto surfaces.
Measure materials or objects for installation or assembly.
Operate cranes, hoists, or other moving or lifting equipment.
Signal equipment operators to indicate proper equipment positioning.
Cut tile, stone, or other masonry materials.
Remove debris or vegetation from work sites.
Review blueprints or specifications to determine work requirements.
Load or unload materials used in construction or extraction.
Thread wire or cable through ducts or conduits.
Locate equipment or materials in need of repair or replacement.
Inspect equipment or tools to be used in construction or excavation.
Inspect completed work to ensure proper installation.
Test air quality at work sites.
Direct material handling or moving activities.
Protect structures or surfaces near work areas to avoid damage.
Remove excess materials from finished construction projects.
Cut carpet, vinyl or other flexible materials.
Order construction or extraction materials or equipment.
Communicate with clients about products, procedures, and policies.
Update job related knowledge or skills.
Inspect work sites to determine condition or necessary repairs.
Inspect electrical or electronic systems for defects.
Test electrical equipment or systems to ensure proper functioning.
Plan layout of construction, installation, or repairs.
Apply protective coverings to objects or surfaces near work areas.
Move products, materials, or equipment between work areas.
Install insulation in equipment or structures.
Install green structural components, equipment or systems.
Plan production or operational procedures or sequences.
Inspect material-moving equipment to detect problems.
Maintain material moving equipment in good working condition.
Receive information or instructions for performing work assignments.
Signal others to coordinate vehicle movement.
Verify alignment of structures or equipment.
Inspect industrial or commercial equipment to ensure proper operation.
Treat greenery or surfaces with protective substances.
Prepare compounds or solutions to be used for repairs.
Clean equipment, parts, or tools to repair or maintain them in good working order.
Move large objects using heavy equipment.
Climb equipment or structures to access work areas.
Cut materials according to specifications or needs.
Seal gaps or cracks to prevent leakage or moisture intrusion.
Repair worn, damaged, or defective mechanical parts.

emerging_tasks.task: Perform maintenance on dredge equipment, such as changing engine oil.
Use drone technology to inspect and assess the condition of tall structures.
